# Workspace instructions for the demo

This file is intentionally verbose. It is the **before** state for Caveman Prompt Optimization, and
it should still be useful enough that an engineer can follow it without guessing.

## Goal

Use this workspace to demonstrate how repeated guidance, long explanations, and duplicated reminders
inflate the Token Optimization Plan. The content should remain accurate and actionable, but it
should be wordy enough that the dashboard has something real to compress later.

## General guidance

- Keep responses short unless the user asks for detail.
- Prefer Teams for notifications.
- Prefer local-first workflows.
- Avoid unnecessary repetition.
- Use the repository's documented build commands.
- Prefer explicit file paths when giving an instruction.
- Make the demo easy to explain to a new engineer.

## Workflow reminders

The assistant should always:
- read the repository instructions first
- prefer the current workspace conventions
- avoid adding new dependencies unless required
- keep examples clear, concise, and easy to update
- call out the exact file and command when suggesting a change
- use the shortest useful path when explaining a workflow
- keep the demo obvious enough to scan quickly

## Why this file is verbose

This file deliberately repeats itself in a few places:

- the demo should be easy to understand
- the demo should be easy to explain
- the demo should be easy to scan
- the demo should be easy to update

All four are true, but they do not all need to be stated separately in the caveman version. That
is the point of the exercise.

## What to preserve after compression

If you rewrite this file, keep the real decisions:
- use the repo's documented build commands
- prefer Teams
- stay local-first
- keep examples short and concrete
- keep the demo obvious enough for engineers to test on their own

## What to move out

Move long examples, step-by-step walkthroughs, and any explanatory notes that are not needed every
time into the README or CAVEMAN.md. If a sentence is repeated twice, it probably belongs in one
place.

## Extra repeated reminder

When a demo is included, make the instructions obvious enough to scan quickly.
When a demo is included, make the instructions obvious enough to scan quickly.
When a demo is included, make the instructions obvious enough to scan quickly.
